title: Resume Optimization for Tech Roles
description: ATS-friendly resume tips, formatting guidelines, and content strategies for software engineering and tech roles.

formatting.rule: Keep to 1 page (2 pages only if 10+ years of highly relevant experience)
Use a clean, single-column layout — ATS systems struggle with multi-column formats
Use standard section headers: Experience, Education, Skills, Projects
Use a standard font (Calibri, Arial, Helvetica) at 10-11pt
Save as PDF unless the application specifically requests .docx
No photos, graphics, or icons — they confuse ATS parsers
Include your name, email, phone, LinkedIn, and GitHub at the top

tips: Lead each bullet with a strong action verb (Designed, Implemented, Optimized, Led, Reduced)
Use the XYZ formula: Accomplished [X] as measured by [Y] by doing [Z]
Quantify everything: 'Reduced API latency by 40%' not 'Improved API performance'
Focus on impact, not just tasks — what was the business outcome?
Tailor bullets to the job description — mirror their language and requirements
Include 3-5 bullets per role, focusing on most impactful work
Mention specific technologies used in context: 'Built a real-time pipeline using Kafka and Flink'
Show progression: increasing scope, leadership, and complexity over time
List languages, frameworks, databases, tools, and cloud platforms
Order by proficiency — strongest first
Match keywords from the job description exactly (React vs React.js matters for ATS)
Don't list soft skills here — demonstrate them in your experience bullets
Include both specific tools (PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka) and categories (distributed systems)
Remove outdated technologies unless specifically relevant to the role

contentStrategy.section: Projects (for earlier career or career changers)
tips: Include 2-3 significant personal or open-source projects
Link to live demos and GitHub repos
Describe the project's purpose, your role, technologies used, and impact/scale
Open-source contributions to well-known projects are highly valued
Include degree, university, and graduation year
GPA only if > 3.5 and you're within 3 years of graduation
Relevant coursework only if you lack professional experience in that area
Include relevant certifications (AWS, GCP, Kubernetes) — they help with ATS

atsOptimization: Use exact keywords from the job description — ATS matches on keywords
Spell out acronyms at least once: 'Amazon Web Services (AWS)'
Avoid tables, text boxes, headers/footers — ATS may not parse them
Use standard bullet points (not custom symbols)
Include the job title you're applying for somewhere in your resume
Don't use 'References available upon request' — it wastes space
Many companies use Greenhouse, Lever, or Workday — test your resume with a free ATS scanner

exampleBullets.weak: Worked on the backend team to improve performance
exampleBullets.strong: Reduced API response latency by 60% (p99 from 800ms to 320ms) by implementing Redis caching and query optimization, improving conversion rates by 12%

exampleBullets.weak: Built a new feature for the mobile app
exampleBullets.strong: Designed and shipped the in-app messaging feature used by 2M+ monthly active users, leading a team of 3 engineers from RFC to launch in 8 weeks

exampleBullets.weak: Responsible for maintaining the CI/CD pipeline
exampleBullets.strong: Migrated CI/CD from Jenkins to GitHub Actions, reducing build times from 45 min to 12 min and enabling 3x more daily deployments
